name: Troll Weaponsmith
concept: You are a troll, and trolls make the finest weapons in the world — everyone knows it, which is why the elves let you stay even though half of them will never trust a troll. You came to the Hollow-Oak as an outsider and, slowly, became something like kin. Your hammer keeps the tribe armed against a world that is closing in.
duty: Your craft is why they keep you, and never quite why they trust you. Every blade you forge is a bond you're building, one strike at a time.

look.label: Build
options: surprisingly deft for all the bulk

look.label: Bearing
options: prouder of the work than of anything

look.label: Craft-mark
options: a maker's brand on every blade

look.label: Eyes
options: softer than the face lets on

gear.label: Tool-weapon
options: a cleaver of your own making (d8)

gear.label: Keepsake
options: the first blade you ever made
a debt of iron owed by another troll
a token from the elf who first spoke for you

moves.name: At the Forge
moves.trigger: When you forge, mend, or improve arms and gear at a hot fire.

results.success: You make or mend the thing the tribe needs, and it is good work.
results.failure: It won't come right — bad metal, a hidden flaw, or the work costs more than you have.
advantage: Hold 1 Craftwork (spend: a masterwork edge — +1 damage or a boost die with it / a hidden feature built in / mend it fast, right now / enough to arm several of the tribe).
You learn something true about the metal, or about who made a blade before you.
threat: It needs rare materials, or more time than you'll easily find.
The fire, the noise, or the smoke draws unwanted eyes.
results.triumph: A masterwork that will outlive you and become a story the tribe tells.
results.despair: The work fails and wastes precious metal, or the forge-fire brings danger down on the Holt.

hold.name: Craftwork
spend: A masterwork edge: +1 damage, or a boost die when the piece is used.
A hidden feature — a concealed blade, a catch, a mark only you know.
Mend it fast, right now, in the middle of trouble.
Turn out enough to arm several of the tribe at once.

moves.name: Troll-Made
moves.trigger: Because trolls make the finest weapons, and you are a troll among elves:
moves.text: Your work is prized and famous; a blade of your making opens doors and earns respect. But your outsider-ness is never forgotten — some elves will always see the troll first and the kin second, and other trolls may name you a traitor for making arms for elves.

moves.name: Troll-Strong
moves.trigger: When you put your massive strength to a task — a fight, a burden, a barred way.

results.success: Sheer strength carries the moment: the foe reels, the weight moves, the way opens.
results.failure: Even you meet something that won't give, and it costs you.
advantage: Do it and keep something in reserve.
Your show of force makes lesser things think about fleeing.
threat: You strain something, or break what you meant to move.
results.triumph: You do what no elf could, and they will not forget it.
results.despair: You overreach, and the failure is as big as you are.

moves.name: Hard to Hurt
moves.trigger: When blows, cold, or hardship that would fell an elf come down on your troll-tough hide.

results.success: You shrug it off and keep standing.
results.failure: Even troll-hide has its limit, and you've found it.
advantage: Shield a smaller ally behind your bulk.
Set a wall the tribe can rally to.
threat: You take it now and stiffen up for it later.
results.triumph: You wade through what should have stopped you cold.
results.despair: Something gets through even you, and badly.

moves.name: Know Your Metal
moves.trigger: When you examine a made thing — a blade, a lock, a mechanism, a hoard.

results.success: Ask the GM one: who made this and how well? what is it worth? where is it weak?
results.failure: It fools even your eye, and that costs you.
advantage: You know how to break it, or improve it.
threat: Handling it, you triggered or marked it.
results.triumph: You read the whole history and secret of the thing.
results.despair: You misjudge a made thing badly, and stake something on it.

bonds: ____ was the first elf to speak for me, and I would forge a blade for their hand before any other.
____ still watches me like the troll I am, and I am slowly, stubbornly proving them wrong.
I am teaching ____ the craft, though trolls are not meant to share it.
____ carries a blade of my making, and while they hold it I feel bound to keep them alive.
I owe ____ for a kindness shown to an outsider who had earned none.
communityTie: The Hollow-Oak Holt keeps you for your hammer; ____ is the one who keeps you for yourself.
When the tribe must fight, they fight with your work in their hands; ____ makes sure you know it matters.
Some in the Holt would still send the troll away; ____ stands between you and them.

basic: Take a move from another playbook.
Take a move from another playbook.
Increase your maximum held Craftwork by 1.
Learn a lost forging: name a kind of masterwork (troll-steel, a shaped fitting) only you can make.
Train a move you lean on: it upgrades 2 dice to proficiency (never more than its stat).
Win a doubter: turn one elf who distrusts the troll into a friend.
advanced: Raise one stat by 1 (max 5). Rare, and only at a true turning point.
Become truly kin: the tribe names you one of their own, troll or not.
Raise a great forge: found a Bastion (a smithy) that makes the Holt a power others come to for arms.
